oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Case based antibiotic selection for various bovine diseases: Where evidence-based veterinary medicine meets spectrum of care
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Dorn, Corale M.
oai_dc:dc.dc:subject: evidence-based medicine
spectrum of care
antimicrobial stewardship
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: In clinical practice, veterinarians are routinely called on to make antimicrobial decisions. Given the wide range of bovine diseases, scope of available antimicrobials, and sometimes lack of evidence-based veterinary medicine reviews that align with a case, veterinarians can feel challenged deciding which drug to use. This paper reviews various factors that should be consid­ered and communication tools that could be used to formulate an acceptable treatment plan with clients.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; No. 1 (2026): 2026 Recent Graduate Conference Proceedings; 13-16
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
